---
title: "`r params$report_title`"
author: "`r params$report_author`"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output:
  html_document:
    theme: flatly
    toc: false
    code_folding: hide
params:
  # Master Data Path
  data_path: !r here::here("1_data", "master_micro_survey.csv")

  # Variables (Using Endline Scores to show final impact)
  outcome_var: "endline_score"
  group_var: "treatment_group"

  # Policy-Facing Labels
  treatment_label: "Received Program"
  control_label: "Comparison Group"
  metric_name: "Endline Test Score"

  # --- Report Text (all editable; leave "" to use the smart default) ---
  report_title: "Treatment Effects: Forest Plot"
  report_author: "Ibraheem Saqib Ellahi"
  chart_title: ""
  chart_subtitle: ""
  x_label: ""
  y_label: ""
  source_note: "Generated via CERP Analytics Template"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  echo = TRUE,
  warning = FALSE,
  message = FALSE,
  dev.args = list(bg = "#FAF9F6"),
  fig.path = paste0(here::here("4_output", "figures"), "/"),
  fig.width = 9,
  fig.height = 3.5,
  fig.retina = 3
)
```

```{r cerp-style, echo=FALSE, results='asis'}
cat("<style>", readr::read_file(here::here("2_R", "cerp_style.css")), "</style>")
```

```{r load-and-prep}
library(tidyverse)

source(here::here("2_R", "2.5_helpers.R"), local = TRUE)

# Load data (headers/values whitespace-scrubbed) and validate YAML params.
# All data scrubbing + plotting now lives in viz_forest() (2_R/2.6_viz_functions.R).
raw_data <- cerp_load(params$data_path)
cerp_validate(raw_data, params[grepl("_var$", names(params))])
```


```{r plot-effects}
source(here::here("2_R", "2.0_setup_theme.R"), local = TRUE)
source(here::here("2_R", "2.6_viz_functions.R"), local = TRUE)

# Thin wrapper: map the YAML params into viz_forest() and print the ggplot it
# returns. Empty-string text params keep the auto-generated defaults.
viz_forest(
  data            = raw_data,
  outcome_var     = params$outcome_var,
  group_var       = params$group_var,
  treatment_label = params$treatment_label,
  control_label   = params$control_label,
  metric_name     = params$metric_name,
  chart_title     = params$chart_title,
  chart_subtitle  = params$chart_subtitle,
  x_label         = params$x_label,
  y_label         = params$y_label,
  source_note     = params$source_note
)
```
